name: BPC-157
stackingNotes: Commonly stacked with TB-500 for enhanced recovery

citations.title: BPC 157 and its effects on healing

description: Gastric pentadecapeptide that promotes healing of tendons, ligaments, muscles, and the GI tract.

name: TB-500
stackingNotes: Pairs with BPC-157 for synergistic tissue repair
description: 43-amino acid peptide that upregulates actin to promote cell migration, tissue repair, and angiogenesis throughout the body.

name: TB4-FRAG
stackingNotes: More targeted than full TB-500; anti-fibrotic properties useful for cardiac and lung tissue
description: Active fragment of Thymosin Beta-4 with potent anti-fibrotic and tissue-repair properties, particularly for cardiac and pulmonary tissue.

name: GHK-Cu
stackingNotes: Pairs with BPC-157 for systemic repair; topical form popular for skin and hair
description: Naturally occurring copper tripeptide that stimulates collagen, promotes wound healing, reduces inflammation, and has broad regenerative effects on skin and tissue.

name: KPV
stackingNotes: Pairs with BPC-157 for gut inflammation; also used topically for skin conditions
description: Anti-inflammatory tripeptide derived from alpha-MSH that inhibits NF-kB signaling; used for IBD, skin inflammation, and systemic inflammation.

name: PEG-MGF
stackingNotes: Use post-workout for localized muscle repair; pairs with IGF-1 LR3

citations.title: Mechano growth factor splice variant in muscle repair

description: PEGylated splice variant of IGF-1 that activates satellite cells for muscle repair and growth; PEGylation extends half-life for less frequent dosing.

name: Collagen Peptides
stackingNotes: Take with Vitamin C for enhanced collagen synthesis; pairs with BPC-157 for tendon/joint repair

citations.title: Collagen peptides improve knee joint symptoms

description: Hydrolyzed collagen protein providing bioactive peptides that stimulate collagen synthesis in joints, skin, tendons, and gut lining.

name: Pentadecapeptide BPC
stackingNotes: Arginate salt form stable for oral dosing targeting upper GI tract

citations.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 in IBD trials

description: Stable oral salt form of BPC-157 optimized for GI tract delivery; used for GERD, gastric ulcers, esophagitis, and leaky gut.

name: Sermorelin
stackingNotes: GHRH analog; pairs with Ipamorelin for synergistic GH release without hunger sides
description: GHRH analog (first 29 amino acids) that stimulates natural pulsatile growth hormone release; used for anti-aging, recovery, and sleep quality improvement.

name: Wolverine Peptide
stackingNotes: Newer peptide; often stacked with BPC-157 and TB-500 for comprehensive repair
description: Emerging regenerative peptide named for its tissue-repair properties; promotes accelerated wound healing and tissue regeneration through multiple pathways.

name: Semaglutide
cycleProtocol: Titrate: 250mcg x4wks, 500mcg x4wks, up to 2400mcg; continuous
stackingNotes: GLP-1 agonist; start low to minimize nausea; can pair with Tirzepatide at lower doses

citations.title: Once-weekly semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity

description: GLP-1 receptor agonist that reduces appetite, slows gastric emptying, and improves insulin sensitivity; FDA-approved for obesity (Wegovy) and T2D (Ozempic).

name: Tirzepatide
cycleProtocol: Titrate from 2.5mg; increase every 4 weeks to max 15mg
stackingNotes: Dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist; more effective than semaglutide in head-to-head trials; start low

citations.title: Tirzepatide once weekly for treatment of obesity

description: Dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist showing up to 22.5% body weight loss in trials; FDA-approved as Mounjaro (T2D) and Zepbound (obesity).

name: AOD-9604
stackingNotes: Modified HGH fragment that burns fat without affecting blood sugar or growth; pairs with CJC/Ipamorelin
description: Modified fragment of HGH (amino acids 177-191) with a tyrosine addition that stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis without affecting IGF-1 or insulin.

name: CJC-1295 DAC
stackingNotes: Long-acting GHRH; pairs with Ipamorelin for synergistic GH pulse; DAC version gives sustained elevation
description: Modified GHRH analog with Drug Affinity Complex that binds albumin, extending half-life to 8 days; provides sustained GH elevation for fat loss and recovery.

name: Ipamorelin
stackingNotes: Cleanest GHRP — no hunger or cortisol sides; best paired with CJC-1295 for synergistic GH release
description: Selective growth hormone secretagogue that stimulates GH release without affecting cortisol, prolactin, or appetite; considered the cleanest GHRP available.

name: Tesamorelin
stackingNotes: FDA-approved GHRH analog; specifically targets visceral fat; emerging data on cognitive benefits

citations.title: Tesamorelin reduces visceral fat in HIV

description: FDA-approved GHRH analog that specifically reduces visceral adipose tissue; also shows cognitive benefits through GH-mediated IGF-1 increase.

name: MOTS-c
aliases: Mitochondrial ORF of Twelve S c
stackingNotes: Mitochondrial-derived peptide; activates AMPK like exercise; pairs with Berberine and NMN
description: Mitochondrial-derived peptide that activates AMPK and regulates metabolic homeostasis; acts as an exercise mimetic improving insulin sensitivity and fat oxidation.

name: Retatrutide
stackingNotes: Triple agonist (GLP-1/GIP/Glucagon); showed 24% weight loss in Phase 2 — most effective obesity drug tested
description: Triple hormone receptor agonist (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon) showing unprecedented 24% body weight loss in Phase 2 trials; most effective anti-obesity peptide in development.

name: HGH Fragment 176-191
stackingNotes: Take fasted for best effect; pairs with AOD-9604 for enhanced lipolysis
description: C-terminal fragment of human growth hormone responsible for its fat-burning properties; stimulates lipolysis without affecting blood sugar or IGF-1 levels.

name: 5-Amino-1MQ
stackingNotes: NNMT inhibitor that prevents NAD+ depletion; pairs with NMN for comprehensive NAD+ support
description: Small molecule NNMT inhibitor that prevents NAD+ degradation, increasing cellular energy metabolism and promoting fat cell shrinkage without stimulant effects.

name: Epitalon
stackingNotes: Pairs with Thymalin in Russian longevity protocols; 10-day courses reactivate telomerase
description: Tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) that activates telomerase to extend telomeres; central to Russian peptide bioregulator longevity protocols showing lifespan extension in human studies.

name: NMN
stackingNotes: NAD+ precursor; pair with TMG to support methylation; Sublingual bypasses first-pass metabolism
description: Direct NAD+ precursor that restores cellular NAD+ levels declining with age; improves energy metabolism, DNA repair, and mitochondrial function.

name: Nicotinamide Riboside
stackingNotes: Alternative NAD+ precursor to NMN; better studied in human clinical trials

citations.title: Nicotinamide riboside augments NAD+ in humans

description: NAD+ precursor vitamin (form of B3) that elevates blood NAD+ levels; better human clinical trial data than NMN with proven safety profile.

name: NAD+ IV
cycleProtocol: Loading: daily x5 days; maintenance: monthly
stackingNotes: Bypasses oral absorption issues; popular in longevity clinics; expensive ($500-1000/session)

citations.title: NAD+ metabolism and its roles in cellular processes

description: Direct intravenous NAD+ infusion bypassing digestive absorption; used in longevity clinics for rapid NAD+ restoration, addiction recovery, and cognitive enhancement.

name: Resveratrol
stackingNotes: Sirtuin activator; pairs with NMN for synergistic NAD+/sirtuin pathway activation (Sinclair protocol)
description: Polyphenol sirtuin activator found in red grapes; activates SIRT1 for cellular stress resistance and longevity pathway signaling; core of Sinclair longevity protocol.

name: Metformin
cycleProtocol: Continuous; skip on heavy exercise days (may blunt mTOR-mediated gains)
stackingNotes: AMPK activator; skip on training days to avoid blunting hypertrophy; Berberine is a natural alternative

citations.title: Metformin as a tool to target aging

description: First-line diabetes drug repurposed as an anti-aging intervention; activates AMPK, improves insulin sensitivity, and is the subject of the TAME (Targeting Aging with Metformin) clinical trial.

name: Rapamycin
cycleProtocol: Pulsed: 3-6mg once weekly; 8 weeks on, 4 weeks off
stackingNotes: mTOR inhibitor; do NOT combine with continuous Metformin (opposite pathways); weekly pulsing mimics caloric restriction

citations.title: Rapamycin fed late in life extends lifespan

description: mTOR inhibitor that mimics caloric restriction signaling; extends lifespan in every organism tested; used in pulsed weekly dosing for anti-aging in the longevity community.

name: Fisetin
cycleProtocol: Senolytic protocol: high-dose 2 days/month; low-dose daily for antioxidant
stackingNotes: Most potent natural senolytic; pairs with Quercetin; take with fat for absorption
description: Flavonoid senolytic that selectively clears senescent zombie cells; most potent natural senolytic identified, also providing antioxidant and neuroprotective benefits.

name: Spermidine
stackingNotes: Autophagy inducer; complementary to rapamycin (different autophagy pathways); pairs with Fisetin
description: Polyamine that induces autophagy (cellular cleanup) through a different pathway than rapamycin; extends lifespan in model organisms and shows cardiovascular benefits in humans.

name: SS-31
stackingNotes: Targets cardiolipin in inner mitochondrial membrane; pairs with CoQ10 and NMN for comprehensive mito support
description: Mitochondria-targeted peptide that binds cardiolipin in the inner membrane to restore electron transport chain efficiency and reduce oxidative stress.

name: Humanin
stackingNotes: Mitochondrial-derived peptide like MOTS-c; neuroprotective; pairs with SS-31 for mitochondrial optimization
description: Mitochondrial-derived peptide with potent neuroprotective and cytoprotective effects; protects against Alzheimer's-related toxicity and improves insulin sensitivity.

name: CoQ10
stackingNotes: Essential if on statins; pairs with SS-31 and NMN for mitochondrial stack
description: Mitochondrial electron transport chain carrier and fat-soluble antioxidant; declines with age and statin use; ubiquinol form has superior bioavailability.

name: Alpha-Lipoic Acid
stackingNotes: Universal antioxidant; R-ALA form is more bioavailable; take away from minerals
description: Universal antioxidant that regenerates glutathione, vitamin C, and vitamin E; improves insulin sensitivity and provides neuroprotection; both water- and fat-soluble.

name: TMG
stackingNotes: Methyl donor preventing SAM-e depletion from NAD+ precursors; essential companion to NMN
description: Methyl donor that prevents SAM-e depletion when using NAD+ precursors; reduces homocysteine and supports liver methylation pathways.

name: Taurine
stackingNotes: 2023 Nature study showed taurine deficiency drives aging; pairs with creatine for performance

citations.title: Taurine deficiency as a driver of aging

description: Conditionally essential sulfonic acid; 2023 Nature study showed taurine supplementation extended healthy lifespan in multiple organisms; highest concentrations in heart and muscle.

name: Semax
stackingNotes: Pairs with Selank for anxiolytic + cognitive combo; NASA variant (N-Acetyl Semax Amidate) is more potent
description: Synthetic ACTH analog peptide (4-10 fragment) used intranasally for cognitive enhancement, BDNF upregulation, and neuroprotection; developed in Russia as a prescription nootropic.

name: Selank
stackingNotes: Anxiolytic peptide; pairs with Semax for balanced mood + cognition stack; GABA-modulating
description: Synthetic analog of tuftsin with anxiolytic and nootropic properties; modulates GABA, serotonin, and dopamine systems while boosting immune function; Russian prescription peptide.

name: Dihexa
stackingNotes: 10 million times more potent than BDNF at HGF receptor; use with extreme caution; very potent at low doses
description: Small molecule angiotensin IV analog that is 10 million times more potent than BDNF at activating HGF/c-Met receptor for neuroplasticity, synaptogenesis, and memory formation.

name: Noopept
stackingNotes: 1000x more potent than Piracetam by weight; pairs with Alpha-GPC for choline support
description: Dipeptide nootropic 1000x more potent than Piracetam by weight; enhances memory consolidation, increases NGF and BDNF, and provides neuroprotection via antioxidant mechanisms.

name: Lion's Mane
stackingNotes: Stimulates NGF; pairs with Noopept and Alpha-GPC for comprehensive cognitive stack

citations.title: Lion's mane stimulates nerve growth factor

description: Medicinal mushroom that stimulates nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis through hericenones and erinacines; improves cognitive function, mood, and may support nerve regeneration.

name: Alpha-GPC
cycleProtocol: Continuous or cycled 5 days on, 2 off
stackingNotes: Best choline source for brain; essential when using racetams or Noopept to prevent headaches
description: Most bioavailable choline source that crosses the blood-brain barrier to increase acetylcholine synthesis; also increases power output in athletes.

name: Bacopa Monnieri
stackingNotes: Slow-building memory enhancer; take with fat for absorption; pairs with Lion's Mane and Alpha-GPC

citations.title: Bacopa monnieri chronic effects on cognition

description: Ayurvedic adaptogenic herb that enhances memory consolidation and recall through bacosides; requires 8-12 weeks of daily use for full cognitive benefits.

name: P21
stackingNotes: CNTF-mimetic that promotes neurogenesis; pairs with Semax for dual neurotrophic support
description: Small peptide derived from CNTF that promotes hippocampal neurogenesis and improves memory; acts via BDNF pathway without CNTF's appetite-suppressing side effects.

name: Cerebrolysin
cycleProtocol: 10-20 days on, 2-3 months off; clinical use for stroke/TBI
stackingNotes: Porcine brain-derived peptide complex; clinical use in stroke recovery; pairs with Semax for comprehensive neuroprotection
description: Porcine brain-derived peptide complex containing neurotrophic factors; used clinically for stroke recovery, traumatic brain injury, and neurodegenerative conditions.

name: Kisspeptin-10
stackingNotes: Pairs with Gonadorelin or Enclomiphene for comprehensive HPG axis support
description: Hypothalamic peptide stimulating GnRH release to drive LH/FSH and testosterone production; used to restore HPG axis function after TRT or during fertility protocols.

name: PT-141
stackingNotes: Melanocortin receptor agonist for central arousal; FDA-approved as Vyleesi
description: Melanocortin receptor agonist acting centrally to increase sexual desire and arousal; FDA-approved as Vyleesi for hypoactive sexual desire disorder.

name: Gonadorelin
stackingNotes: Mimics GnRH directly; maintains testicular function during TRT
description: Synthetic GnRH stimulating pituitary LH and FSH secretion; used to maintain testicular size, sperm production, and fertility during TRT.

name: Enclomiphene
stackingNotes: Pairs with Gonadorelin for comprehensive HPG axis stimulation
description: Selective estrogen receptor modulator (trans-isomer of clomiphene) that increases LH and FSH by blocking negative estrogen feedback, raising testosterone while preserving fertility.

name: DHEA
stackingNotes: Foundational hormone; pairs with Pregnenolone; declines ~2% per year after 30
description: Precursor to testosterone and estrogen declining ~2% per year after 30; supplementation improves mood, energy, libido, and lean body mass.

name: Pregnenolone
stackingNotes: Upstream precursor to all steroid hormones; pairs with DHEA
description: Master neurosteroid precursor to all steroid hormones; also acts directly on NMDA and GABA receptors to enhance memory, mood, and cognitive function.

name: HCG
cycleProtocol: 250-500 IU 2x weekly with TRT; 1000-2000 IU 3x weekly for PCT
stackingNotes: Direct LH mimetic; maintains testicular volume during TRT
description: LH analog stimulating testicular Leydig cells to produce testosterone and maintain testicular size and spermatogenesis during TRT.

name: Testosterone Cypionate
stackingNotes: Foundation of TRT; pair with AI if estradiol elevated; combine with HCG/Gonadorelin for fertility
description: Long-acting testosterone ester; most widely prescribed TRT formulation providing stable serum levels. Requires prescription.

name: Progesterone
stackingNotes: GABA-A modulation improves sleep; essential balance to estrogen in HRT
description: Bioidentical hormone and neurosteroid modulating GABA-A receptors for sleep and anxiety; essential for hormonal balance in women and increasingly used by men.

name: L-Glutamine
stackingNotes: Foundational gut repair; pairs with BPC-157 and Zinc Carnosine
description: Conditionally essential amino acid and primary fuel for intestinal enterocytes; maintains tight junction integrity and reduces gut permeability.

name: Zinc Carnosine
stackingNotes: Pairs with L-Glutamine; take away from other minerals

citations.title: Zinc carnosine stabilises small bowel integrity

description: Chelated zinc-carnosine complex stabilizing gastric mucosa and promoting ulcer healing; approved in Japan for gastric ulcer treatment.

name: Berberine
stackingNotes: Nature's Metformin; activates AMPK; take with food to reduce GI upset
description: Plant alkaloid activating AMPK to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism while modulating gut microbiota; natural alternative to Metformin.

name: Bovine Colostrum
stackingNotes: Pairs with L-Glutamine and BPC-157 for comprehensive gut healing
description: First-milk extract containing IGF-1, lactoferrin, and immunoglobulins that strengthen gut lining, reduce permeability, and enhance immune function.

name: Butyrate
stackingNotes: Short-chain fatty acid fuel for colonocytes; tributyrin form has better absorption; pairs with fiber for natural butyrate production
description: Short-chain fatty acid that is the primary fuel for colonocytes; strengthens gut barrier, reduces inflammation, and modulates immune function in the colon.

name: Slippery Elm
stackingNotes: Demulcent that coats and protects gut lining; pairs with Zinc Carnosine for comprehensive mucosal support
description: Mucilaginous bark that forms a protective coating over the GI lining to soothe inflammation, reduce acid reflux, and support mucosal healing.

name: MK-677
cycleProtocol: 3-6 months on, 1 month off; monitor fasting glucose
stackingNotes: Oral GH secretagogue; can raise blood sugar — monitor glucose; pairs with Berberine to offset glucose impact
description: Oral growth hormone secretagogue mimicking ghrelin to stimulate pulsatile GH release; increases IGF-1 and improves sleep quality; can increase appetite and blood glucose.

name: IGF-1 LR3
stackingNotes: Potent anabolic; can cause hypoglycemia — monitor blood sugar; pairs with HGH for synergistic growth
description: Extended half-life variant of IGF-1 that promotes muscle hyperplasia (new fiber creation) and hypertrophy; 2-3x more potent than native IGF-1.

name: GHRP-2
stackingNotes: Strong GH release but increases appetite and cortisol; GHRP-6 increases appetite more; Ipamorelin is cleaner
description: Growth hormone releasing peptide that stimulates GH secretion via the ghrelin receptor; moderate appetite increase and mild cortisol elevation.

name: GHRP-6
stackingNotes: Strongest appetite stimulation of all GHRPs — useful for hard gainers; GHRP-2 or Ipamorelin better if appetite unwanted
description: First-generation GHRP that potently stimulates GH release and appetite through the ghrelin receptor; strongest hunger effect of all GH secretagogues.

name: Hexarelin
stackingNotes: Most potent GHRP but desensitizes GH receptors after ~12 weeks; cycle strictly; cardioprotective properties
description: Most potent growth hormone releasing peptide with unique cardioprotective properties; causes receptor desensitization after prolonged use requiring strict cycling.

name: Follistatin-344
stackingNotes: Myostatin inhibitor; very expensive; results are dramatic but short-lived without ongoing use

citations.title: Follistatin gene therapy for muscle growth

description: Glycoprotein that binds and inhibits myostatin, the protein that limits muscle growth; enables muscle growth beyond normal genetic limits.

name: HGH
cycleProtocol: 6+ months continuous; 2-4 IU anti-aging, 4-8 IU performance
stackingNotes: Gold standard GH; pairs with Ipamorelin for pulsatile release; expensive; monitor IGF-1 and blood sugar
description: Recombinant human growth hormone used for anti-aging, body composition, recovery, and performance; requires prescription and regular IGF-1 monitoring.

name: Creatine Monohydrate
stackingNotes: Most evidence-backed supplement; pairs with MK-677 for lean mass; emerging cognitive and neuroprotective data
description: Phosphocreatine precursor regenerating ATP during high-intensity exercise; most evidence-backed performance supplement with emerging cognitive and longevity benefits.

name: CJC-1295/Ipamorelin Combo
stackingNotes: The classic GH peptide combo — CJC amplifies GHRH signal while IPA triggers the GH pulse; synergistic effect
description: Synergistic combination of GHRH analog (CJC-1295) and GHRP (Ipamorelin) that amplifies natural pulsatile GH release for fat loss, muscle growth, and improved sleep.

name: DSIP
stackingNotes: Combines with Melatonin and Glycine for comprehensive sleep stack
description: Nonapeptide promoting slow-wave delta sleep, reducing cortisol, and modulating circadian rhythms.

name: Melatonin
stackingNotes: Start at 0.5-1mg; higher causes grogginess; pairs with Magnesium and L-Theanine
description: Pineal gland hormone signaling circadian timing; potent antioxidant with anti-aging properties; biohackers use lowest effective dose (0.5-1mg).

name: Glycine
stackingNotes: Lowers core body temperature for sleep onset; pairs with Magnesium L-Threonate
description: Inhibitory neurotransmitter improving sleep quality by lowering core body temperature and modulating NMDA receptors.

name: Magnesium L-Threonate
cycleProtocol: Continuous; cognitive benefits build over 4-6 weeks
stackingNotes: Only magnesium crossing BBB; pairs with Glycine and DSIP
description: Brain-bioavailable magnesium crossing the blood-brain barrier to restore synaptic magnesium; improves sleep quality and cognitive function.

name: L-Theanine
frequency: Before bed or morning with caffeine
stackingNotes: Synergizes with caffeine for focused calm; pairs with Magnesium Glycinate for sleep
description: Green tea amino acid promoting alpha-wave brain activity for relaxed alertness without sedation; reduces anxiety and improves sleep quality.

name: Ashwagandha
stackingNotes: KSM-66 extract is best-studied; pairs with Magnesium for sleep
description: Adaptogenic root reducing cortisol and improving sleep quality; evidence for modest testosterone elevation with KSM-66 extract.

name: Magnesium Glycinate
stackingNotes: Best-tolerated magnesium for sleep; pairs with Melatonin and L-Theanine
description: Highly bioavailable chelated magnesium calming the nervous system and improving sleep onset and quality without morning grogginess.

name: Thymosin Alpha-1
cycleProtocol: Continuous for chronic conditions; 4-8 week courses for acute support
stackingNotes: Gold standard immune peptide; FDA orphan drug; pairs with Thymalin and LL-37
description: Thymic peptide enhancing T-cell maturation, dendritic cell function, and NK cell activity; FDA orphan drug status for hepatitis B; used in cancer immunotherapy protocols.

name: LL-37
cycleProtocol: 2-4 weeks for acute; 4-8 weeks for chronic
stackingNotes: Human antimicrobial peptide; disrupts biofilms; pairs with Thymosin Alpha-1 for comprehensive immune support
description: Human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide with broad-spectrum activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi; unique ability to disrupt biofilms.

name: Thymalin
stackingNotes: Pairs with Epitalon in Russian longevity protocols
description: Thymus-derived polypeptide complex restoring immune function and promoting T-cell maturation; central to Russian peptide bioregulator longevity protocols.

name: Zinc
stackingNotes: Essential cofactor; pair with copper at doses >25mg/day; take with Vitamin D3
description: Essential mineral cofactor for 300+ enzymes; deficiency impairs immune function, testosterone synthesis, and wound healing.

name: Vitamin D3
stackingNotes: Always pair with K2 (100-200mcg MK-7); test levels before supplementing
description: Fat-soluble hormone regulating immune function, calcium metabolism, and testosterone synthesis; deficiency affects 40%+ of the population.

name: Quercetin
cycleProtocol: Continuous; or pulsed with Fisetin for senolytic
stackingNotes: Synergistic with Fisetin for senolytics; zinc ionophore properties
description: Flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory, senolytic, and zinc ionophore properties; widely studied for immune modulation and longevity.

name: Omega-3 Fish Oil
stackingNotes: Foundation supplement; target 2g+ combined EPA/DHA; pairs with Vitamin D3 and Quercetin
description: Essential fatty acids EPA and DHA with potent anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular, and neuroprotective effects; foundation supplement for biohackers.

name: Vitamin K2
cycleProtocol: Continuous; always pair with Vitamin D3
stackingNotes: Essential companion to Vitamin D3; prevents arterial calcification by directing calcium to bones
description: Fat-soluble vitamin activating matrix GLA protein to prevent arterial calcification; essential companion to Vitamin D3 supplementation.

name: NAD+ Nasal Spray
stackingNotes: Bypasses first-pass metabolism; cheaper than IV; pairs with NMN oral for sustained levels
description: Intranasal NAD+ delivery bypassing digestive degradation for direct brain uptake; more accessible alternative to IV NAD+ infusions.

name: Apigenin
stackingNotes: CD38 inhibitor preserving NAD+ levels; Huberman recommends 50mg before bed; pairs with NMN
description: Flavonoid from chamomile that inhibits CD38 enzyme to preserve NAD+ levels while promoting sleep through mild anxiolytic effects; Huberman Lab recommendation.

name: Tongkat Ali
stackingNotes: Huberman-popularized; standardized to 2%+ eurycomanone; pairs with Fadogia Agrestis (use with caution)

citations.title: Eurycoma longifolia for male sexual health

description: Southeast Asian herb that reduces SHBG to increase free testosterone, improves cortisol:testosterone ratio, and enhances libido; popularized by Andrew Huberman.

name: Fadogia Agrestis
cycleProtocol: 8 weeks on, 2-4 weeks off; monitor liver enzymes
stackingNotes: Pairs with Tongkat Ali per Huberman protocol; cycle strictly due to potential testicular toxicity at high doses in animal studies
description: Nigerian shrub extract stimulating testicular steroidogenesis; pairs with Tongkat Ali in Huberman protocol; requires cycling due to limited long-term safety data.
